title: A Farewell to False Love by Sir Walter Raleigh
content:
Farewell, false love, the oracle of lies,
A mortal foe and enemy to rest,
An envious boy, from whom all cares arise,
A bastard vile, a beast with rage

title: Song of Myself by Sir Walter Raleigh
content:
I was a Poet!
But I did not know it,
Neither did my Mother,
Nor my Sister nor my Brother.
The Rich were not aware of it;
The Poor took no care of it.

title: Life by Sir Walter Raleigh
content:
What is our life? A play of passion,
Our mirth the music of division,
Our mother's wombs the tiring-houses be,
Where we are dressed for this short com

title: Farewell to the Court by Sir Walter Raleigh
content:
Like truthless dreams, so are my joys expir'd,
And past return are all my dandled days;
My love misled, and fancy quite retir'd--
Of all which pass'd

title: Nature that Washed Her Hands in Milk by Sir Walter Raleigh
content:
Nature, that washed her hands in milk,
And had forgot to dry them,
Instead of earth took snow and silk,
At love's request to try them,
If she a mistre
